step_by_step_thinking:
You are allowed to call retrieve() multiple times if needed.
We are evaluating which natural disaster causes the *maximum* number of deaths globally. The previous discussion has centered on both fire-related burns and flooding/hydrological impacts, with conflicting conclusions pointing toward either A (hydrological) or D (fires). However, our task is to determine objectively which category leads to the greatest death toll across all recorded historical data—not limited to clinical injury profiles alone.
Therefore, I need reliable epidemiologic evidence comparing fatality rates among different categories of natural disasters.
First step: Gather comprehensive global data on natural disaster mortality trends.

We are evaluating which natural disaster causes the *maximum* number of deaths globally. The previous discussion has centered on both fire-related burns and flooding/hydrological impacts, with conflicting conclusions pointing toward either A (hydrological) or D (fires). However, our task is to determine objectively which category leads to the greatest death toll across all recorded historical data—not limited to clinical injury profiles alone.
Therefore, I need reliable epidemiologic evidence comparing fatality rates among different categories of natural disasters.
First step: Gather comprehensive global data on natural disaster mortality trends.
